story_prompt: make a lecture on js class

scenes.text: In JavaScript, classes act as templates for creating objects. They encapsulate data with code to work on that data. Although JavaScript is prototype-based, classes give a more intuitive, object-oriented syntax, which resembles class-based code structures in other programming languages like Java or C#. This makes code more readable and maintainable, thus allowing developers to easily implement inheritance and encapsulation, crucial for software design patterns.

JavaScript's class syntax supports inheritance, allowing one class to inherit properties and methods from another. This inheritance is implemented using the 'extends' keyword, providing a way to create a hierarchy of classes and reuse code efficiently. This feature is essential for creating complex applications efficiently, as it enables developers to build on existing code without duplication.

JavaScript classes include methods that define actions the objects created from the class can perform. The use of static methods and getters/setters further enhances class functionality. Static methods are called on the class itself, whereas getters and setters offer a way to intercept property access and assignments, making class usage more intuitive and safer by providing controlled access to properties.
